content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 23, 1990

L. G. Statham
1008 Tenth Street, #514
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-064

Dear Ms. Statham:

As treasurer for Assemblyman Stan Statham, you have requested
advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act.1/

QUESTION

How does a candidate who has two bank accounts determine
which account to use when paying certain types of expenses?

CONCLUSION

Expenditures for fundraising, campaign strategy, mass
mailings, and other communications in connection with a future
election, voter registration, and expenses for campaign consulting
services or campaign staff must be paid from the 1990 campaign
account. Other expenses related to political, governmental, or
legislative activities may be paid out of either account.

FACTS

Assemblyman Stan Statham has two bank accounts, one
established prior to the passage of Proposition 73, and a campaign
bank account established for the 1990 election. You have provided
a list of various types of expenses and have asked which expenses
should be paid from each account.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

L. G. Statham
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Regulation 18525 (copy enclosed) specifies the types of
expenditures which are considered to be in connection with a
future election and must be paid from a bank account established
for that election. Offeholder or other expenses related to
political, governmental, or legislative activities not covered
under Regulation 18525 may be paid from either a campaign bank
account established for a current term of office or from a
campaign account established for election to a future term of the
same office.

Please note that effective January 1, 1990, amendments to the
Act placed certain restrictions on the use of campaign funds.
(Sections 85800-85807.) Enclosed is a fact sheet which summarizes
the new provisions.

If you have any additional questions please feel free to
contact this office at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
